title: Quelev Tapper
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Quelev_Tapper.jpg]] |name=Quelev Tapper |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] (43:11 GrS),''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' establishes a 12 [[Standard month/Legends|month]] [[Standard year/Legends|year]], replacing the old 10 month year format established by [[West End Games]]. [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Obroa-skai information raid|raid]] on [[Obroa-skai/Legends|Obroa-skai]] from ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' is dated at 44:4:15, and concurrently, [[Talon Karrde]] mentions that [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] has been with his [[Car'das/Legends|organization]] for five-and-half months. ''[[First Contact]]'' establishes that Tapper [[Death/Legends|died]] on [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]] during the same encounter that saw Jade recruited by Karrde. Subtracting this figure from the date given for Obroa-skai yields a date of 43:11. [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]]{{JournalCite|1|First Contact}} |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]]When ''[[First Contact]]'' was reprinted on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|''Hyperspace'']], the existing black and white image of Tapper by [[Mike Vilardi]] used in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]] was colored. |feathers= |eyes=Green |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Quelev Tapper's organization]] *[[Car'das/Legends|Talon Karrde's organization]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|This is Quelev Tapper for Karrde. We've gotten the initial payment for this lot and you've got fifty million credits still in your account. In another month we should have another thirty percent of your order ready.|Quelev Tapper, to Wedge Antilles|}} '''Quelev Tapper''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who operated during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Formerly the head of his own [[Quelev Tapper's organization|organization]], Tapper agreed to merge with fellow smuggler [[Talon Karrde]]'s smuggling [[Car'das/Legends|group]] after [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces [[Destruction of Quelev Tapper's headquarters|leveled]] his [[Quelev Tapper's headquarters|headquarters]] and destroyed three quarters of his operation. Tapper became Karrde's second-in-command and most trusted [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], and his conservative personality countered Karrde's more flamboyant nature. The pair would often personally investigate new business opportunities. In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], Tapper accompanied Karrde to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kaal/Legends|Kaal]] to investigate the rumor that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] personal [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]], the [[Emperor's Shadow (shuttle)|''Emperor's Shadow'']], had crash-landed in the world's [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]]. Due to Imperial intervention, Tapper was forced to detonate the wreck to save Karrde, a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] delegation, and Kaal's ruler, [[Tirgee Benyalle]]. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], a deal was struck between Karrde and former smuggler [[Booster Terrik]] to supply ex–New Republic [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Antilles's Rogues|Rogue Squadron]] with munitions during their [[Bacta War|war]] against [[Ysanne Isard]], who had taken control of the [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartels]] on [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]]. Tapper commanded the [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] that traveled to [[the Graveyard]] in the [[Alderaan system/Legends|Alderaan system]] to transfer the equipment and was caught in a [[Second ambush in the Graveyard|battle]] between the Rogues and forces loyal to Isard. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Tapper accompanied Karrde to [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]] to investigate the operations of the [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Gamgalon]]. While working undercover, the pair were discovered by Gamgalon and his Krish underlings, who planned to [[Death/Legends|kill]] them. Tapper tried to escape, but was killed during the attempt. Karrde was rescued by former [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]], [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], who was posing as a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] named Celina Marniss. ==Biography== ===Imperial troubles=== {{Quote|As for this asteroid, Tapper found it, but before he could exploit it he ran into some Imperial problems. After our groups merged, he brought it to my attention. We're using it until we find something more suitable.|Talon Karrde explains the origin of his asteroid base to Booster Terrik|}} Quelev Tapper, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], led a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] [[Quelev Tapper's organization|organization]] that operated during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Tapper's group profited immensely from the subsequent collapse of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. However, by [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], his operations were firmly within Imperial-held territory, and a [[Governor/Legends|governor]] learned the location of Tapper's [[Quelev Tapper's headquarters|headquarters]]. An Imperial force was sent to [[Destruction of Quelev Tapper's headquarters|destroy]] the smuggler's base. Smuggler [[Chief/Legends|chief]] [[Talon Karrde]] was in the area conducting business, and came to Tapper's aid. Tapper and a quarter of his organization survived the Imperial assault; Tapper and Karrde agreed to a merger, and Tapper folded his organization into Karrde's [[Car'das/Legends|operation]]. In turn, Tapper became Karrde's second-in-command and most trusted [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], and the two would often check out potential business ventures together.{{JournalCite|7|The Kaal Connection}} ===Adventures on Kaal=== [[File:KarrdeasHart.png|thumb|left|Talon Karrde, posing as Syndic Pandis Hart on Kaal]] During 6 ABY, Karrde heard a rumor that a [[Starship/Legends|ship]] belonging to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself, the [[Emperor's Shadow (shuttle)|''Emperor's Shadow'']], had crash-landed on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kaal/Legends|Kaal]] in the [[Yushan sector/Legends|Yushan sector]]. The rumor also said that the ship was equipped with a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]]. Karrde and Tapper traveled to Kaal to investigate; while Karrde posed as [[Syndic/Legends|Syndic]] Pandis Hart, chief purchasing agent of the [[Sif-Uwana Council]], Tapper purchased a [[Submarine/Legends|submersible]] craft from a local [[Unidentified dealer|dealer]] and began searching for the [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] near the [[Unis Islands]]. Karrde, posing as Hart, entered into negotiations with [[Tirgee Benyalle]], the ruler of Kaal. Benyalle was selling the rights to buy and harvest food from Kaal, and had attracted delegations from the Galactic Empire, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Utoxx Prentioch]]. While the other three were negotiating for the full rights to Kaal's food production, Karrde only planned to request that the Sif-Uwana Council be allowed to conduct its own harvesting operation in a specific area. Tapper's role was to locate the wreck of the yacht, allowing Karrde to define an area for the deal so that he could recover the vessel in secret. Tapper's search bore fruit, and he located the yacht submerged near the Unis Islands and relayed its location to Karrde. Tapper remained on station while Karrde negotiated with Benyalle. Shortly after, Karrde communicated with Tapper, and told him to rig the ship to blow. Tapper complied, setting charges throughout the wreck. The reason for Karrde's order soon became clear; the negotiations had fallen apart when Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Uther Kermen]] had declared his intention to take the planet and its resources by force. The delegations were meeting aboard [[Tirgee Benyalle's luxury sail barge]], touring the current harvesting operations, when Kermen made his move. Over the course of several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]], Kermen had positioned his own [[Agent/Legends|agents]] amongst the [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge's]] crew, and they attempted to take control of the vessel at his signal. Karrde had allied himself with the New Republic delegation, who had saved Benyalle from Kermen's attack. The New Republic took control of the sail barge, and Karrde steered them toward the Unis Islands and parked them above the crashed yacht. Tapper surfaced the yacht, taking on Karrde, Benyalle, the New Republic delegation, and the remaining loyal members of the sail barge's crew. The submersible sped away from the sail barge as the charges that Tapper had planted on the yacht detonated, destroying the wreck and the sail barge above. With his reasons for being on Kaal eliminated, Karrde pulled out of negotiations with Benyalle and allowed the New Republic to cut a deal with her. ===Arming the Rogues=== {{Quote|Perhaps he wants ''Another Chance'' or the Death Star's womb. I mean, as long as your aim is to break the Bacta Cartel, you might as well go in for other things you can't get.|Quelev Tapper, to Booster Terrik, on Wedge Antilles' chances of breaking the Bacta Cartel|}} Prior to Tapper's Imperial troubles, he had learned the location of a [[Talon Karrde's asteroid base|hollowed-out asteroid]] that had been mostly stripped of [[Ore/Legends|ore]], but the assault on his organization had occurred before he had a chance to exploit his find. Once he had joined Karrde, he informed the latter about the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]], and Karrde set up a temporary base within its confines. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Karrde and Tapper met with [[Booster Terrik]], a former smuggler who was operating on behalf of [[Antilles's Rogues|Rogue Squadron]] [[Rogue Leader/Legends|leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]. Antilles, along with the rest of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], had resigned his [[Military rank/Legends|commission]] in the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|military]] following the news that the New Republic would not interfere when [[Ysanne Isard]]—formerly in command of the Imperial forces based from the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]—took control of the planet [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]] and its [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartels]]. Cut off from official New Republic support, Antilles and the Rogues fought a [[Bacta War|private war]] against Isard, and needed supplies. Terrik, a long-time family friend of Antilles', acted as a middle-man in procuring [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]], [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]], and their respective [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] packages and launchers. A deal was struck wherein Karrde would supply the required equipment in exchange for [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] that Rogue [[Squadron/Legends|Squadron]] had "liberated" from [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]] controlled by Isard. Karrde appointed one of his associates, [[Melina Carniss]], to liaise with Terrik; Carniss was a former employee of the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], and Tapper was smitten with the woman. As the conversation came to a close, Tapper made an off-hand comment regarding attempting to locate the legendary [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] armory ship ''[[Another Chance]]'' for Antilles, or the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] that had constructed the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], since finding either seemed as likely as destroying the Bacta Cartel with a handful of [[Snubfighter/Legends|snubfighters]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]]. Terrik pointed out that the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] had, in fact, recovered the ''Another Chance'' prior to the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], and not to bet against Antilles. Tapper apologized, meaning no offense. [[File:The_Graveyard.jpg|thumb|right|The Graveyard of Alderaan]] As part of a regular delivery of equipment, Tapper commanded one of Karrde's freighters, the ''[[Starry Ice]]'', and led a convoy of a half-dozen ships to rendezvous with Rogue Squadron and their allies to transfer munitions. The meeting was set to take place near [[the Graveyard]] in the [[Alderaan system/Legends|Alderaan system]]; Rogue Squadron and their [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] and [[Gand/Legends|Gand]] allies were covering a dozen freighters under Booster Terrik's command, led by the ''[[Pulsar Skate]]''. As the transfer was about to begin, Isard's [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] ''[[Aggregator]]'' and [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Corrupter/Legends|''Corrupter'']] decanted from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. As the freighters scattered, Antilles led the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] [[Second ambush in the Graveyard|against]] the Imperial force. The resulting battle ended in the destruction of the ''Corrupter'', and the retreat of the ''Aggregator''. Antilles called for the surrender of the surviving [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and the various [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] from the ''Corrupter'', which was accepted. Antilles arranged with Tapper to recover the escape pods and extract any ransom he deemed fit from the survivors. Tapper was also interested in acquiring the surrendered TIE fighters, but Antilles refused to hand them over, classifying them as [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] of war. Tapper relented, noting that Antilles would soon be buying spare parts for the captured starfighters from Karrde. Antilles was worried that increasing security on Karrde's deliveries to them in the wake of the attack in the [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] graveyard would cause him to cancel the arrangement, but Terrik was sure that Tapper would be able to convince Karrde that they remained trustworthy. It was later discovered that the location of the meeting had been sold to Isard by Melina Carniss. Karrde and Terrik arranged for Carniss to leak the location of the [[Yag-prime|Rogue's base]] at [[Yag'Dhul/Legends|Yag'Dhul]], thereby precipitating the [[Battle of Thyferra|final battle]] in the Bacta War that brought down Isard. ===Hunting on Varonat=== {{Quote|What a fine, civilized-looking place this is. You sure we didn't overshoot and land in someone's weed dump?''"
"''No, this is it. The Great Jungle of Varonat. Home of a handful of third-rate trading depots and a few thousand colonists who haven't the brains to pick up and go elsewhere.''"
"''And an ugly Krish named Gamgalon. I don't know, Karrde. I still think we should have brought in the ''Wild Karrde'' and ''Starry Ice'' and had some decent firepower behind us. We're kind of like sitting mynocks here.|Quelev Tapper and Talon Karrde discuss the pleasures of Varonat|}} [[8 ABY/Legends|A year later]], Tapper accompanied Karrde to the planet [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]], located on the [[Ison Trade Corridor/Legends|Ison Corridor]], aboard the yacht ''[[Uwana Buyer]]''. Karrde had heard that a [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]], [[Gamgalon]], was using [[Morodin/Legends|Morodin]]-[[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] safaris as a cover for illegal activities. Curious as to the nature of the business and whether or not it was something on which Karrde could cut himself in, he again posed as Syndic Pandis Hart, while Tapper took on the role of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Seoul, his [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. Feigning [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] trouble, the ''Uwana Buyer'' landed on Varonat, in the settlement of [[Tropis-on-Varonat]]. There, the pair enlisted the services of a hyperdrive [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] to repair the drive on the ''Uwana Buyer''—which had been expertly gimmicked by one of Karrde's associates, [[Chin]]—and allowed Gamgalon's [[come-up flector]], [[Fleck (Human)|Fleck]], to "convince" them that they should join one of Gamgalon's safaris. Karrde agreed, and Tapper, playing the role of hired employee, made a case that he should be allowed to join Karrde on the hunt. Paying 17,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to join the safari, Tapper and Karrde availed themselves of the services of [[Great Jungle Outfitters]], a shop that catered to supplying the safari hunters with every need. [[File:Varonat.jpg|thumb|left|The planet Varonat]] Returning to the ''Uwana Buyer'', Tapper commented that he hoped that their shopping trip had given Gamgalon's people enough [[Time/Legends|time]] to search the ship to determine if they were a threat or not. Investigating a noise from the aft section of the ship, Tapper and Karrde found a [[Mara Jade Skywalker|young woman]] working on the hyperdrive. She introduced herself as Celina Marniss; her knowledge about the cause of their hyperdrive failure, along with her general attitude and demeanor, impressed Tapper and Karrde. In addition, her name gave them cause to think there was more to her than met the [[Eye/Legends|eye]]. They had previously known a "Melina Carniss," and this name of "Celina Marniss" was awfully coincidental.''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' After a night's [[Sleep/Legends|sleep]], Tapper and Karrde joined the safari expedition. Led by a Krish named [[Falmal]], the group consisted of two other guides—the Human [[Buzzy]] and the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Jombo]]—and four other hunters—two [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] by the names of [[Hav]] and [[Jivis]]; a [[Thennqora]] called [[Tamish]]; and [[Cob-caree]], a [[Saffa (species)|Saffa]]. Loading into [[Aratech Repulsor Company/Legends|Aratech]] [[Arrow-17 airspeeder]]s, Tapper and Karrde were accompanied by Falmal as the party headed for a base camp in the jungle. From there, the expedition hiked through the jungle for several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] to make camp near to the Morodin hunting area. During a rest break, one of the hunters, Tamish, noticed that a Morodin had passed through the area at some point as a patch of ground had been discolored by the slime trail produced by Morodins. Tapper and Karrde made note of a series of [[Plant/Legends|plants]] growing neatly along the slime mark, as well as a piece of metal embedded in the ground that Tapper tentatively identified as a [[transpond marker]]. Before they could investigate further, Falmal told them that the plants were non-native [[Yagaran aleudrupe plant]]s that were the result of careless hunters dropping the seeds of [[Aleudrupe berry/Legends|aleudrupe berries]] during a hunt, then briskly moved them on. Several hours later, after erecting their camp, Tapper and Karrde discussed their fellow hunters and came to conclusion that they were not fellow smugglers in disguise as they were, tromping through the jungle for clandestine meetings with Gamgalon, but rather wealthy, bored beings looking for adventure. Initially, Karrde had been of the opinion that the safaris were merely a way for Gamgalon to conduct clandestine meetings away from prying eyes. Karrde then posited the idea that they could have been legitimate businessbeings with whom Gamgalon wanted to deal, rather than criminals, but Tapper pointed out that confidential meetings could be held in other places with no trouble. As they started to discuss the transpond marker and whether or not it was part of a larger sensor array, a low growl, distinctive of the Morodin, was heard from the jungle near them. Falmal ordered the party to split into the same groups that had travelled together on the airspeeders, so Tapper and Karrde remained with the Krish. Following him into the jungle, they followed the Morodin slime trails and encountered one of the creatures. Falmal urged Karrde to shoot it, but Karrde hesitated because the beast remained still, and he advised Tapper to hold fire as well. From the side, Tamish and Cob-caree, accompanied by Jombo, came out of the forest and [[Death/Legends|killed]] the creature. Over the next two hours, pilots and airspeeders shuttled meat and trophies from the kill back to base camp. Tapper helped the process, doing both his and Karrde's share of the work, while Karrde retreated from the group and isolated himself with a [[Melodium/Legends|melodium]] and began to fiddle with the [[comm-relay]] hidden inside the device in an attempt to locate Gamgalon's transpond markers. Tapper joined his boss when the work was done, commenting that many in the camp thought Karrde was sulking. Karrde told Tapper what he had been doing, and led him into the jungle. Immediately, the comm-relay picked up a signal, which they followed to the site of the recent Morodin kill, discovering that the earth around the slime trail had been freshly cultivated. Hearing a noise from the direction of the camp, they attempted to take the long way back, but ended up deeper in the jungle. On a hunch, Tapper suggested heading toward a ridge that overlooked a depression that he remembered seeing on a map of the area. Reaching the ridge, they discovered a valley and a herd of fifty Morodins. Observing them for a short while, Tapper and Karrde came to the conclusion that the beasts were, in fact, a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. [[File:Gamgalon.jpg|thumb|right|The Krish, Gamgalon, who was responsible for Tapper's death]] Gamgalon and a group of Krish discovered Tapper and Karrde, having been tipped off by Falmal's suspicions about the pair. Gamgalon proceeded to explain his operation to Tapper and Karrde; the Morodin slime initiated certain chemical changes in the pips of aleudrupe berries that, when combined with [[rethan-K]] and [[promhassic triaxli]], created a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] gas formulation as powerful as spin-sealed [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]]. All three components were also perfectly legal to transport and would not arouse the suspicions of customs officials. The safaris were merely a way of covering the planting and harvesting of the berries, and Gamgalon bribed the [[Unidentified Imperial governor (Ison Corridor)|local Imperial governor]] to cover the fact that he was hunting a sentient species. Gamgalon could allow no word of his operation to be leaked, although Karrde attempted to reach an agreement with him. Tapper grabbed the [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] out of the [[Limb/Legends|hands]] of one of the Krish, jammed it into the guard's torso, then dove for the cover of a [[Tree/Legends|tree]]. Bringing the rifle to bear on Gamgalon and Falmal, Tapper was struck by a pair of blaster bolts fired by another Krish concealed along the ridge. With a gasp, Tapper died. Karrde was subsequently rescued by Celina Marniss, who distracted Gamgalon and Falmal with [[Recorder|recorded]] Morodin growls and then killed the pair. As Karrde and Marniss left the planet on the ''Uwana Buyer'', Karrde revealed his true identity to her. Marniss did the same, introducing herself as Mara Jade. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Good point, but I suspect Quelev Tapper can convince Karrde that we're trustworthy.|Wedge Antilles|}} Quelev Tapper was a light-skinned male, with [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and green eyes. Slender and handsome, he was a physically imposing individual. Tapper maintained a serious demeanor and was always business-like. A naturally suspicious man, he was the perfect counter to Talon Karrde's polite grace and often assumed the role of "[[Hypothetical Hrongar]]" when Karrde outlined his more elaborate ideas. Tapper had infrequent hunches that usually turned out to be worth following up, and Karrde soon learned to trust his associate's instincts. Like Karrde, he shared the opinion that idealists caused a lot of trouble in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Being a more cautious individual, Tapper preferred to have substantial backup available when entering an unknown situation. Tapper knew how to handle a blaster and usually armed himself with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]]. He was also skilled in [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee]] and [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]], and carried a [[Knife/Legends|knife]]. He was a skilled pick pocket, and could be stealthy when he needed to be. Tapper had knowledge of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species and [[Language/Legends|languages]], [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]], [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracies]], and businesses. He could pilot small vessels and [[Astrogation/Legends|navigate]] them through hyperspace, and was trained to operate the weapons and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] aboard such vessels. He could also repair the ships he was trained to pilot. Tapper could also drive [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles and submersibles, and operate communications, security and sensor equipment. He also had some familiarity with explosives. A savvy businessman, Tapper was adept at brokering deals by either persuasion or by [[Con artist/Legends|conning]] them, and was well aware of the value of the items in which he dealt. He carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] with him at all times. ==Behind the scenes== Quelev Tapper first appeared in ''[[First Contact]]'' in [[1994]], a short story written by [[Timothy Zahn]] for the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]''. Supplementary game information in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] section accompanying the story was contributed by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]], and the image of Tapper was provided by [[Mike Vilardi]]. ''First Contact'' was subsequently reprinted in ''[[Tales from the Empire]]'' and on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]]. When the story was reprinted on ''Hyperspace'', the original black-and-white image of Tapper was colorized. Tapper later appeared in ''[[The Kaal Connection]]'' in [[1995]], which was written by Schweighofer with Zahn. ''The Kaal Connection'' was later reprinted in ''[[Classic Adventures: Volume Four — The Best of the Journal]]''. Tapper's only other appearance was in ''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'', written by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and published in [[1997]]. Tapper has since been mentioned in other articles and reference works, usually when describing Talon Karrde's or Mara Jade's histories. However, in many cases, the sources disagree with the original source over certain facts. In 1995's ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'', Tapper is referred to as "Tapper Quelev," and the work states that Tapper's organization joined Karrde's shortly after the Battle of Endor, and that Tapper worked with Karrde for four years before his death, contradicting ''First Contact''{{'s}} assertion that Tapper had been with Karrde for only two years. In [[The Smugglers Alliance|''The Smuggler's Alliance'']] article in [[Star Wars Gamer 3|''Star Wars Gamer'' 3]] published in [[2001]], it states Tapper was killed on "Tropis-on-Varonat," rather than simply "Varonat." The former was the name of a settlement on the planet. In ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'', released in [[2002]], Tapper is mentioned in Karrde's entry. Yet the entry places the merging of Karrde's and Tapper's organizations at four years after the Battle of Endor, which equates to 8 ABY. As ''The Kaal Connection''—the events of which are also referenced in Karrde's article—shows, Tapper was working with Karrde by 6 ABY, two years earlier. Karrde's entry also places Tapper's death at six and a half years after Endor, in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. As the [[Thrawn campaign|Thrawn Crisis]] depicted in Timothy Zahn's [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|Thrawn Trilogy]] was placed at [[9 ABY/Legends|five years after]] the Battle of Endor, and Tapper was dead before then as Mara Jade was referenced as serving with Karrde's organization for five and half months by the opening of the first book in the trilogy, ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', these references are incorrect. Tapper is mentioned twice in ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]''. Firstly, in Mara Jade Skywalker's entry in ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 27]]'', he is simply referred to as a "safari hunter." The entry goes on to state that Jade was living on the planet "Tropis" at the time, rather than Varonat, and that she rescued both Talon Karrde and his companion, when, in fact, Tapper died on the planet. In Talon Karrde's entry in ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 50]]'', Tapper is constantly referred to as "Tapper Quelev." It also states that Tapper's organization merged with Karrde's four years prior to his death after Karrde rescued him shortly after the Battle of Endor, instead of the two years stated in ''First Contact''. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|7|The Kaal Connection|reprint=1}} *''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: The Bacta War (unabridged audiobook)}} *{{JournalCite|1|First Contact|reprint=1}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{JournalCite|14|From the Files of Corellia Antilles}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|Under ''Karrde, Talon''}} *{{GamerCite|3|The Smugglers Alliance}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{FactFile|27|MAR 1-4|Mara Jade Skywalker}} {{Imo}} *{{FactFile|50|KAR 1-4|Talon Karrde}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|talonkarrde|Karrde, Talon}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tapper, Quelev}} [[Category:Car'das members]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]] [[Category:Smugglers]]
